arabic_source.entry:
٩

مَدَنِيَّة وَلَا نَظِير لَهَا فِي عَددهَا
أخبرنَا خلف بن إِبْرَاهِيم قَالَ أَنا أَحْمد بن مُحَمَّد قَالَ أَنا عَليّ بن عبد الْعَزِيز قَالَ أَنا الْقَاسِم بن سَلام قَالَ أَنا هشيم عَن أبي بشر عَن سعيد بن جُبَير قَالَ قلت لِابْنِ عَبَّاس سُورَة التَّوْبَة فَقَالَ تِلْكَ الفاضحة مَا زَالَت تنزل وَمِنْهُم وَمِنْهُم حَتَّى خشينا أَن لَا تدع أحدا
أخبرنَا فَارس بن أَحْمد قَالَ أَنا أَحْمد بن مُحَمَّد قَالَ أَنا أَحْمد بن عُثْمَان قَالَ أَنا الْفضل بن شَاذان أَنا نوح بن أنس أَنا جرير عَن الْأَعْمَش عَن عَمْرو بن مرّة عَن عبد الله بن سَلمَة عَن حُذَيْفَة قَالَ إِنَّكُم تسمون هَذِه السُّورَة سُورَة التَّوْبَة وَإِنَّهَا سُورَة الْعَذَاب وَالله مَا تركت أحدا إِلَّا نَالَتْ مِنْهُ أهل الْمَدِينَة يسمونها التَّوْبَة وَأهل مَكَّة الفاضحة
وكلمها أَلفَانِ وَأَرْبع مئة وَسبع وَتسْعُونَ كلمة
وحروفها عشرَة آلَاف وثماني مئة وَسَبْعَة وَثَمَانُونَ حرفا
وَهِي مئة وتسع وَعِشْرُونَ آيَة فِي الْكُوفِي وَثَلَاثُونَ فِي عدد البَاقِينَ
اختلافها ثَلَاث آيَات (﴿أَن الله بَرِيء من الْمُشْركين﴾) عدهَا الْبَصْرِيّ وَلم يعدها الْبَاقُونَ (﴿إِلَّا تنفرُوا يعذبكم عذَابا أَلِيمًا﴾) وَهُوَ الأول عدهَا الشَّامي وَلم يعدها الْبَاقُونَ (﴿وَعَاد وَثَمُود﴾) عدهَا المدنيان والمكي وَلم يعدها الْبَاقُونَ
وفيهَا مِمَّا يشبه الفواصل وَلَيْسَ معدودا بِإِجْمَاع سِتَّة عشر موضعا
(﴿إِلَّا الَّذين عاهدتم من الْمُشْركين﴾) بعده (﴿ثمَّ لم ينقصوكم﴾) على أَن أهل الْبَصْرَة قد جَاءَ عَنْهُم خلاف فِيهِ وَفِي قَوْله تَعَالَى (﴿بَرِيء من الْمُشْركين﴾) وَالصَّحِيح عَنْهُم مَا قدمْنَاهُ وَهِي رِوَايَة الْمُعَلَّى عَن الجحدري وروى شهَاب عَنهُ أَنه عد الثَّانِي وَلم يعد الأول وَفِي روايتنا عَن ابْن شَاذان عَن الْحلْوانِي عَن عقبَة عَن هَيْصَم عَنهُ أَنه عد الأول وَلم يعد الثَّانِي كَرِوَايَة الْمُعَلَّى عَنهُ وَالَّذِي فِي أول السُّورَة مجمع على عده (﴿وقاتلوا الْمُشْركين﴾ برحمة مِنْهُ ورضوان) (﴿وقلبوا لَك الْأُمُور﴾ وَفِي الرّقاب) (﴿ويؤمن للْمُؤْمِنين﴾ من يَلْمِزك فِي الصَّدقَات) (﴿يعذبهم الله عذَابا أَلِيمًا﴾) وَهُوَ الثَّانِي (﴿مَا على الْمُحْسِنِينَ من سَبِيل﴾ وَلَا على الَّذين لَا يَجدونَ مَا يُنْفقُونَ) (﴿من الْمُهَاجِرين وَالْأَنْصَار﴾ وَتَفْرِيقًا بَين الْمُؤمنِينَ) (﴿فيقتلون وَيقْتلُونَ﴾ أَن يَسْتَغْفِرُوا للْمُشْرِكين) (﴿مَا يَتَّقُونَ﴾ أَنهم يفتنون)

english_translation.entry_translation:
Sūrat al-Tawbah.

Medinan. It has no counterpart (naẓīrah) in its verse-count.

Ibn ʿAbbās was asked about Sūrat al-Tawbah and said: “That is al-Fāḍiḥa. It kept coming down with ‘and among them … and among them …’ until we feared that it would leave no one unmentioned.”

Ḥudhayfa said: “You call this surah Sūrat al-Tawbah, but it is Sūrat al-ʿAdhāb. By God, it did not leave anyone without touching him.” The people of al-Madīna call it al-Tawbah, and the people of Makka call it al-Fāḍiḥa.

Its words are two thousand four hundred and ninety-seven, and its letters are ten thousand eight hundred and eighty-seven. It is one hundred and twenty-nine verses in the Kufi count and one hundred and thirty in the count of the others.

Its points of disagreement are three verse-endings: “anna llāha barīʾun mina l-mushrikīn” is counted by the Basri and not counted by the others; “illā tanfirū yuʿadhdhibkum ʿadhāban alīman,” the first occurrence, is counted by the Shami and not counted by the others; and “wa-ʿĀd wa-Thamūd” is counted by the two Madanis and the Makki and not counted by the others.

In it there are, by consensus, sixteen places that resemble verse-endings without being counted. The first is “illā lladhīna ʿāhadttum mina l-mushrikīn,” identified by the following phrase “thumma lam yanquṣūkum.” At that point the source pauses to note conflicting Basri reports concerning that location and concerning “barīʾun mina l-mushrikīn,” while stating that the sound Basri position is the one already given earlier and that the phrase at the opening of the surah is unanimously counted. The remaining places are: “wa-qātilū l-mushrikīn”; “bi-raḥmatin minhu wa-riḍwān”; “wa-qallabū laka l-umūr”; “wa-fī l-riqāb”; “wa-yuʾminu li-l-muʾminīn”; “man yalmizuka fī l-ṣadaqāt”; “yuʿadhdhibhumu llāhu ʿadhāban alīman,” the second occurrence; “mā ʿalā l-muḥsinīna min sabīl”; “wa-lā ʿalā lladhīna lā yajidūna mā yunfiqūn”; “mina l-muhājirīna wa-l-anṣār”; “wa-tafrīqan bayna l-muʾminīn”; “fa-yaqtulūna wa-yuqtalūn”; “an yastaghfirū li-l-mushrikīn”; “mā yattaqūn”; and “annahum yuftanūn.”
english_translation.ruus_note: The ordinal_madani_2_baseline field gives the recovered later-Madani sequence. In the transmitted witness, the opening head “من الْمُشْركين” is unnumbered, the block then runs with displayed numerals 1–69, inserts an unnumbered “يظْلمُونَ” after displayed 69 “وَثَمُود”, resumes at displayed 70 “حَكِيم”, and ends with a blank terminal display-total line “١٢ - ٩”. The structured data therefore treats the displayed numerals as witness metadata rather than as the primary ordinal layer.

structured_data.source_alignment_note_en: The entry spans printed pages 160–161 and is textually stable, but its sixteen-item pseudo-fawasil list is interrupted after the first item by a prose note on conflicting Basri reports concerning the following phrase and concerning “بَرِيء من الْمُشْركين”. The transmitted رؤوس الآي block on printed 162 is otherwise recoverable as a full later-Madani sequence, but its display numerals are not a clean ordinal layer: the first head “من الْمُشْركين” is unnumbered, an additional unnumbered “يظْلمُونَ” appears after displayed 69 “وَثَمُود”, and the witness ends with a blank terminal total line “١٢ - ٩”.

supporting_transmission_reports.raw_ar: أخبرنَا خلف بن إِبْرَاهِيم قَالَ أَنا أَحْمد بن مُحَمَّد قَالَ أَنا عَليّ بن عبد الْعَزِيز قَالَ أَنا الْقَاسِم بن سَلام قَالَ أَنا هشيم عَن أبي بشر عَن سعيد بن جُبَير قَالَ قلت لِابْنِ عَبَّاس سُورَة التَّوْبَة فَقَالَ تِلْكَ الفاضحة مَا زَالَت تنزل وَمِنْهُم وَمِنْهُم حَتَّى خشينا أَن لَا تدع أحدا
supporting_transmission_reports.chain_ar: خلف بن إِبْرَاهِيم ← أَحْمد بن مُحَمَّد ← عَليّ بن عبد الْعَزِيز ← الْقَاسِم بن سَلام ← هشيم ← أبي بشر ← سعيد بن جُبَير ← ابْن عَبَّاس
supporting_transmission_reports.summary_en: Ibn ʿAbbās calls the surah al-Fāḍiḥa, saying that its repeated “wa-minhum ...” disclosures made them fear it would spare no one.

supporting_transmission_reports.raw_ar: أخبرنَا فَارس بن أَحْمد قَالَ أَنا أَحْمد بن مُحَمَّد قَالَ أَنا أَحْمد بن عُثْمَان قَالَ أَنا الْفضل بن شَاذان أَنا نوح بن أنس أَنا جرير عَن الْأَعْمَش عَن عَمْرو بن مرّة عَن عبد الله بن سَلمَة عَن حُذَيْفَة قَالَ إِنَّكُم تسمون هَذِه السُّورَة سُورَة التَّوْبَة وَإِنَّهَا سُورَة الْعَذَاب وَالله مَا تركت أحدا إِلَّا نَالَتْ مِنْهُ أهل الْمَدِينَة يسمونها التَّوْبَة وَأهل مَكَّة الفاضحة
supporting_transmission_reports.chain_ar: فَارس بن أَحْمد ← أَحْمد بن مُحَمَّد ← أَحْمد بن عُثْمَان ← الْفضل بن شَاذان ← نوح بن أنس ← جرير ← الْأَعْمَش ← عَمْرو بن مرّة ← عبد الله بن سَلمَة ← حُذَيْفَة
supporting_transmission_reports.summary_en: Ḥudhayfa says that people call it Sūrat al-Tawbah, though he calls it Sūrat al-ʿAdhāb; he also reports that the people of al-Madīna call it al-Tawbah and the people of Makka al-Fāḍiḥa.

additional_counting_notes.raw_ar: على أَن أهل الْبَصْرَة قد جَاءَ عَنْهُم خلاف فِيهِ وَفِي قَوْله تَعَالَى (﴿بَرِيء من الْمُشْركين﴾) وَالصَّحِيح عَنْهُم مَا قدمْنَاهُ وَهِي رِوَايَة الْمُعَلَّى عَن الجحدري وروى شهَاب عَنهُ أَنه عد الثَّانِي وَلم يعد الأول وَفِي روايتنا عَن ابْن شَاذان عَن الْحلْوانِي عَن عقبَة عَن هَيْصَم عَنهُ أَنه عد الأول وَلم يعد الثَّانِي كَرِوَايَة الْمُعَلَّى عَنهُ وَالَّذِي فِي أول السُّورَة مجمع على عده
additional_counting_notes.summary_en: The source pauses the pseudo-fawasil list to note conflicting Basri transmissions about the location after “إِلَّا الَّذين عاهدتم من الْمُشْركين” and about “بَرِيء من الْمُشْركين”, while affirming that the sound Basri position is the one already stated earlier and that the phrase at the opening of the surah is unanimously counted.

word_count.raw_ar: أَلفَانِ وَأَرْبع مئة وَسبع وَتسْعُونَ كلمة

letter_count.raw_ar: عشرَة آلَاف وثماني مئة وَسَبْعَة وَثَمَانُونَ حرفا

pseudo_fawasil.locator_note_en: The source identifies this place by the following phrase “ثُمَّ لم ينقصوكم” and immediately interrupts the list with a Basri counting note.

pseudo_fawasil.phrase_ar: وَلَا على الَّذين لَا يَجدونَ مَا يُنْفقُونَ

structured_data.ruus_al_ayat_numbering_note_en: The ordinal_madani_2_baseline field gives the recovered later-Madani sequence. In the transmitted witness, the opening head “من الْمُشْركين” is unnumbered, the block then runs with displayed numerals 1–69, inserts an unnumbered “يظْلمُونَ” after displayed 69 “وَثَمُود”, resumes at displayed 70 “حَكِيم”, and ends with a blank terminal display-total line “١٢ - ٩”. The structured data therefore treats the displayed numerals as witness metadata rather than as the primary ordinal layer.

ruus_al_ayat.extraction_note_en: Unnumbered first head in the transmitted witness; treated as ordinal 1 in the recovered later-Madani sequence.
Unnumbered inserted head after displayed 69 “وَثَمُود”; treated as ordinal 71 in the recovered later-Madani sequence.

extraction_anomalies.description_en: The sixteen pseudo-fawasil are not transmitted as a neat continuous list: after the first item, the source interrupts the sequence with a prose discussion of conflicting Basri reports before resuming the remaining clusters.
extraction_anomalies.impact_en: The sixteen-item extraction is recoverable, but it requires editorial disaggregation and an explicit note about the intervening Basri counting discussion.

extraction_anomalies.description_en: The source records conflicting Basri transmissions involving the location after “إِلَّا الَّذين عاهدتم من الْمُشْركين” and the phrase “بَرِيء من الْمُشْركين”, while also stating that the sound Basri position is the one already presented earlier.
extraction_anomalies.impact_en: The translation and structured data preserve the raw note but avoid over-resolving every internal variant beyond the source’s own preferred summary.

extraction_anomalies.description_en: The first transmitted head, “من الْمُشْركين,” appears without a displayed numeral.
extraction_anomalies.impact_en: The recovered ordinal layer treats this as head 1 while preserving the witness’s lack of a displayed numeral.

extraction_anomalies.description_en: After displayed 69 “وَثَمُود”, the witness inserts an unnumbered “يظْلمُونَ” before resuming at displayed 70 “حَكِيم”.
extraction_anomalies.impact_en: The inserted head must be preserved explicitly as a separate ordinal in the recovered later-Madani sequence.

extraction_anomalies.description_en: The witness ends with a blank terminal display-total line written as “١٢ - ٩”.
extraction_anomalies.impact_en: The terminal line is preserved as a display total only and is not treated as an additional head.

extraction_anomalies.description_en: Because the transmitted block begins unnumbered, inserts an additional unnumbered head after displayed 69, and ends with a blank terminal total line, the displayed numerals do not function as a clean independent ordinal layer.
extraction_anomalies.impact_en: The dataset stores recovered later-Madani ordinals together with witness display numbers where present.
